A wise servant shall have rule over a son that causeth shame, and shall have part of the inheritance among the brethren.
¶ Then the king said to the wise men, which knew the times, (for so was the king’s manner toward all that knew law and judgment:
He that gathereth in summer is a wise son: but he that sleepeth in harvest is a son that causeth shame.
Also Jonathan David’s uncle was a counsellor, a wise man, and a scribe: and Jehiel the son of Hachmoni was with the king’s sons:
And he hath slandered thy servant unto my lord the king; but my lord the king is as an angel of God: do therefore what is good in thine eyes.
A wise servant shall have rule over a son that causeth shame, and shall have part of the inheritance among the brethren.
The wrath of a king is as messengers of death: but a wise man will pacify it.
The king’s wrath is as the roaring of a lion; but his favour is as dew upon the grass.

For I was ashamed to require of the king a band of soldiers and horsemen to help us against the enemy in the way: because we had spoken unto the king, saying, The hand of our God is upon all them for good that seek him; but his power and his wrath is against all them that forsake him.
And if so be that the king’s wrath arise, and he say unto thee, Wherefore approached ye so nigh unto the city when ye did fight? knew ye not that they would shoot from the wall?
Verily I say unto you, notwithstanding their sins, my bowels are filled with compassion towards them. I will not utterly cast them off; and in the day of wrath I will remember mercy.
A wise servant shall have rule over a son that causeth shame, and shall have part of the inheritance among the brethren.
He that gathereth in summer is a wise son: but he that sleepeth in harvest is a son that causeth shame.
Yea, it grieveth my soul and causeth me to shrink with shame before the presence of my Maker, that I must testify unto you concerning the wickedness of your hearts.
He that wasteth his father, and chaseth away his mother, is a son that causeth shame, and bringeth reproach.
A wise servant shall have rule over a son that causeth shame, and shall have part of the inheritance among the brethren.
He that gathereth in summer is a wise son: but he that sleepeth in harvest is a son that causeth shame.

For I was ashamed to require of the king a band of soldiers and horsemen to help us against the enemy in the way: because we had spoken unto the king, saying, The hand of our God is upon all them for good that seek him; but his power and his wrath is against all them that forsake him.
I speak to your shame. Is it so, that there is not a wise man among you? no, not one that shall be able to judge between his brethren?

We ask thee, Holy Father, to confound, and astonish, and to bring to shame and confusion, all those who have spread lying reports abroad, over the world, against thy servant or servants, if they will…
